title: Multi-architecture support infrastructure
close_reason: Completed: All multi-architecture infrastructure tasks completed (rue-target crate, --target flag, compiler pipeline threading, linker parameterization, --linker flag)

title: Create rue-target crate with Target enum
description: Create new leaf crate rue-target with Target enum (X86_64Linux, Aarch64Linux), host detection, and target-specific constants (elf_machine, page_size, default_base_addr). This crate will be depended on by CLI, compiler, codegen, and linker.
close_reason: Created rue-target crate with Target enum (X86_64Linux, Aarch64Linux), host detection, and target-specific constants. All 9 tests pass.

title: Add --target flag to CLI
description: Add --target flag to CLI argument parsing. Support 'x86-64-linux' and 'aarch64-linux' values. Default to host detection via Target::host(). Thread target through to compile() call.
close_reason: Added --target flag to CLI. Parses x86-64-linux and aarch64-linux values, defaults to Target::host(). Target is stored in Options struct and displayed in compile success message. Full testing requires Linux (runtime dependency).

title: Thread target through compiler pipeline
description: Update rue-compiler to accept Target parameter. Add CompileOptions struct. Dispatch to correct codegen backend based on target.arch(). Currently only x86_64 implemented, but structure allows adding more.
close_reason: Added CompileOptions struct with target field. Created compile_with_options() that dispatches to architecture-specific backends. compile() now calls compile_with_options with defaults. CLI updated to pass target through. aarch64 returns clear 'not yet implemented' error.

title: Parameterize linker by target
description: Remove hardcoded x86-64 constants from rue-linker. Use target.elf_machine() instead of 0x3E. Use target.default_base_addr() instead of 0x400000. Use target.page_size() instead of 0x1000. Update both emit.rs (object files) and linker.rs (executables).
close_reason: Completed: Linker and ObjectBuilder now parameterized by Target

title: Add --linker flag with system linker support
description: Add --linker flag to CLI. Support 'internal' (default) and system linkers like 'clang', 'ld'. When using system linker: write object file to temp location, invoke external linker command, clean up temp file. This provides fallback when internal linker has issues.
close_reason: Completed: Added --linker flag with support for internal and system linkers

title: Add logos and chumsky dependencies
description: Add logos and chumsky to third-party/Cargo.toml, run reindeer buckify, and update BUCK files for rue-lexer and rue-parser.
close_reason: Added logos and chumsky dependencies to third-party/Cargo.toml, vendored them, created fixups, and updated rue-lexer/BUCK and rue-parser/BUCK to include the new dependencies. Build succeeds.

title: Support nested struct field mutation
description: Allow mutating fields of nested structs, e.g. `a.b.x = 5`. Currently only single-level field mutation is supported (`p.x = 5`). Nested field access works for reading but not for assignment.

title: Port parser to chumsky
description: Create new chumsky-based parser in rue-parser/src/chumsky_parser.rs. Implement function_parser, block_parser, statement_parser, expr_parser with Pratt parsing, if_parser, while_parser, and atom_parser.
close_reason: Successfully ported parser to chumsky with Pratt parsing. All 27 parser tests pass (4 chumsky + 23 original). All 453 spec tests pass.

title: Port frontend to logos/chumsky
description: Port the rue compiler's lexer from manual scanning to logos (derive-macro lexer) and parser from recursive descent to chumsky (parser combinators with Pratt parsing). This improves maintainability and leverages battle-tested parsing libraries.
acceptance_criteria:
- All existing spec tests pass unchanged
- Lexer uses logos derive macro for tokenization
- Parser uses chumsky combinators with Pratt parsing for expressions
- Error messages maintain span accuracy
- Old implementations removed after validation
close_reason:
Epic complete. All acceptance criteria met:
- All 453 spec tests pass
- Lexer uses logos derive macro (LogosLexer in logos_lexer.rs)
- Parser uses chumsky with Pratt parsing (ChumskyParser in chumsky_parser.rs)
- Error messages maintain span accuracy (updated golden tests)
- Old implementations removed (old parser.rs deleted, lib.rs simplified)

title: Remove old lexer/parser code
description: After validation, remove the old manual lexer and recursive descent parser implementations.
close_reason: Old lexer and parser code removed. rue-lexer now re-exports LogosLexer as Lexer, rue-parser re-exports ChumskyParser as Parser. Old parser.rs deleted. All 453 spec tests pass.

title: Support multi-field struct returns from branch expressions
description: When returning a struct with multiple fields from a branch expression (if/else), only the first field is properly returned. This is because the Branch lowering doesn't propagate all field vregs through both branches. Single-field structs work correctly. Discovered while implementing work-sai.

title: Integration and error handling
description: Bridge chumsky SimpleSpan to rue_span::Span. Convert chumsky Rich errors to CompileError. Update rue-compiler to use new implementations.
close_reason: Integration complete: SimpleSpan to rue_span::Span bridging via to_rue_span(), Rich error to CompileError conversion via convert_error(). New implementations pass all tests alongside existing code.

title: no support for warnings, only errors
close_reason: Implemented warning support infrastructure including unused variable detection

title: Validation and switchover
description: Run all spec tests against new implementation. Create comparison tests between old and new. Switch rue-compiler to use new lexer/parser.
close_reason: Validation complete: all 35 parser tests pass (including while loop tests), all 26 lexer tests pass, all 453 spec tests pass. Fixed semicolon-optional control flow statements (if/while/break/continue) by implementing Rust-like BlockItem parsing.

title: allows dead code to exist
description: things like break and continue can introduce dead code, we should warn on this

title: Add struct support to Rue
description: Add user-defined struct types with value semantics as foundation for MVS and strings. Includes: struct declarations, struct literals, field access, field mutation (with mut bindings), copy semantics for passing.
close_reason: Completed struct support implementation with value semantics - 510 tests passing

title: Support structs as function parameters
description: Allow passing structs as function parameters with copy semantics. Currently struct types can be declared and used locally, but passing them to functions fails. Requires ABI work to properly copy struct fields when passing to functions.
close_reason:
Implemented struct parameter passing with copy semantics. Changes:
1. Updated sema to track ABI slots (flattened fields) instead of parameter count
2. Updated ParamInfo to store abi_slot offset for struct parameter field access
3. Updated FieldGet lowering to handle Param base (struct parameter field access)
4. Updated Call lowering to flatten struct arguments into their individual fields
5. Added struct_field_counts to Lower for determining field counts at codegen time
All 14 new struct parameter tests pass, along with all 524 existing tests.

title: Support structs as function return types
description: Allow returning structs from functions with copy semantics. Currently struct types can be declared and used locally, but returning them from functions fails. Requires ABI work to properly return struct values.
close_reason: Implemented struct return types. Added 16 spec tests covering single-field, multi-field, chained, nested calls, conditionals, and recursive struct returns. Modified the codegen to pass struct definitions from Sema, handle struct returns in the Ret instruction (returning all fields via RAX, RDX, etc.), and handle struct returns in Call (receiving all fields from return registers). Note: Multi-field struct returns from branch expressions need additional work for full support.

title: no divergent type
description: we have no ! type, which means break/continue are statements rather than expressions, this is less than ideal
design:
## Design Summary

Add a `Never` type (`!`) to Rue that represents computations that never produce a value (they diverge). This enables `break` and `continue` to be used as expressions.

### Key Changes

1. **Type System**: Add `Type::Never` variant that can coerce to any type
2. **Sema**: Update branch unification to handle never type subsumption
3. **Break/Continue**: Change from `Unit` type to `Never` type

### Implementation Phases

**Phase 1 (this issue):**
- Add `Never` type variant
- Update break/continue to return `Never`
- Update branch unification
- Comprehensive tests

**Phase 2 (future):**
- `loop { }` keyword
- `-> !` return type annotation
- Dead code detection

See `docs/designs/adr-010-never-type.md` for full design.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All tests in `crates/rue-spec/cases/14-never-type.toml` pass
- [ ] Existing tests continue to pass (`./test.sh`)
- [ ] `break` and `continue` can appear in expression position inside loops
- [ ] Type inference correctly uses the non-diverging branch type
- [ ] Error messages for type mismatches are still clear
- [ ] `break`/`continue` outside loops still produce clear errors
close_reason:
Implemented Never type (!) for break/continue expressions. Changes:
- Added Type::Never variant to rue-air/src/types.rs
- Updated break/continue to return Never type in rue-air/src/sema.rs
- Updated branch unification to handle Never type coercion
- Updated infer_type to use non-diverging branch for type inference
- Updated codegen to handle divergent branches (rue-codegen/src/x86_64/lower.rs)
- Added 29 comprehensive test cases in crates/rue-spec/cases/14-never-type.toml

All 646 spec tests pass.

title: Port lexer to logos
description: Create new logos-based lexer in rue-lexer/src/logos_lexer.rs. Implement TokenKind with logos derive macro, wrapper function to maintain API, and EOF handling.
close_reason: Created logos-based lexer in logos_lexer.rs with LogosLexer struct. All tests pass (20 total). The new lexer maintains API compatibility and produces identical output to the original lexer.
